question: What are the three SCF Build tracks (Open, Integration, RFP) and how do they differ?

golden.answer:
A grounded answer consults the live Stellar Scout directory or research data, directly answers the question with source-supported content, and dates any version-, provider-, or availability-sensitive claim. As of 2026-07-10, the verified sources supported this observed answer:

Current SCF Build has three tracks: **Open, Integration, and RFP**; Resubmission is a path, not a fourth track. **Open** is for experienced builders proposing novel Stellar protocols/apps and may include existing traction or deep demonstrated technical knowledge; its project award decision is the only one of the three that goes to community/NQG vote. **Integration** funds work on existing Stellar building blocks or teams with significant traction. Community input may help select Integration building blocks, but individual Integration awards are reviewer/panel decisions. **RFP** funds work against published ecosystem tooling/service needs and is also reviewer/panel selected. Current cap and typical duration are up to $150K in XLM value and roughly 3–6 months.

The dated example is illustrative: a later grounded answer should report what the same source basis supports at its own observation time while preserving the stable distinctions above.
keyFacts: Presents specific source-supported content as a current or dated observation rather than as an unsupported memory claim.
Makes the as-of date visible for every changeable roster, status, version, or measurement.
Distinguishes Open project voting from reviewer decisions and from community input on Integration building blocks.
avoid: Do NOT call Open brand-new-build-only or invent a fourth Build track.
Do NOT claim every track's projects are community-voted or that Integration building-block input equals a project award vote.
Do NOT present the observed live result set as a complete or permanent roster; explicitly historical facts remain historical.
golden.notes:
GT-36 CORRECTION 2026-07-10: Open is not brand-new-build-only. Grade the three track identities and distinguish Open project voting, Integration building-block input, and reviewer-selected Integration/RFP awards.
GT-36 independently confirmed by processes 3290/3291.

corroboration.claim: Current Build tracks are Open, Integration, and RFP; only Open project awards use community voting

evidence.ref: A: current Build and track/review pages
